description: Binary pass/fail on device-level health flags. Any runtime_bad or uncorrectable error triggers device FAIL regardless of how many blocks remain healthy.
industry_context: Many industrial QA systems apply a binary pass/fail on device health flags. Any runtime_bad flag triggers immediate device DISCARD. The remaining healthy block capacity is not examined.
yieldos_contrast: YieldOS separates remaining capacity into functional zones (safe / approximate_cache / read_only_archive / discard) rather than applying a single pass/fail threshold to the whole device. This enables reclassification into appropriate use cases under human review.
binary_verdict_basis: runtime_bad_count > max_runtime_bad_blocks OR uncorrectable_error_count > max_uncorrectable_error_blocks → device_fail
